# Transcrição: 04 How to Access Business Manager

- **Fonte:** 04 How to Access Business Manager
- **Duração:** 02:51
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:51]

Okay, so once you've set up your business manager and added a page to it and you've also added yourself as someone who can manage that page whether you're an admin or an editor or a moderator, here is how you can access that page or any ad accounts via business manager. So essentially this is how you're going to access business manager. So there's really two ways. One way is to go to the Facebook page. So this here is that test page that I set up and you'll notice that there's something here that wasn't there before and it's this little gray box up here that says go to business manager to manage this page. So if I click on this, there's going to be a very subtle difference. You can see by this top toolbar now we're in business manager. Previously you would have had these tabs anyway as an admin of the page. So all of a sudden it switched to business manager and now I can now boost posts using whatever ad accounts I have. So if I scroll down to this lovely test post, you'll see this boost post buttons now here and I can click that and I can boost this post from whatever ad accounts I have access to within this business manager. So in this case it's only this one, but if you had multiple ad accounts in this business and your business manager account, you'd be able to select from them in this drop down. So I can also track conversions here by turning on the Facebook pixel and making sure that that's set up of course. And I can add a call to action button to my post. You'd have to put the URL in there. So it's a very subtle difference, but I'm now in business manager as well as you can see. So if I wanted to see any ad accounts, all I'd have to do is go to the top left corner and go under my business settings. So you can also access that on this site here. So once I'm in business settings, it's going to take me to the screen here where I can see all of my assets, my people, pages, ad accounts. So I can click in here and then all of my ad accounts would come up. I'd click on the one that I want and then I can view the ad account in ads manager. So this is a brand new ad account which hasn't been set up properly. So that's why it's about to take us to the ad creation page instead of ads manager. The other way to access business manager is to type the URL in straight here. So if I go to business.facebook.com, it's going to take me straight into business manager. So I've got a lot of business manager accounts that I have access to, but when you guys first go there, if you just got the one, this will be the screen that you see.
